questions.text: How many players are on the field for each team in a standard football match?
questions.explanation: Each team fields 11 players, including the goalkeeper, for a total of 22 players on the pitch.

questions.text: How long is a standard football match (excluding extra time)?
questions.explanation: A standard match consists of two 45-minute halves, totalling 90 minutes of regulation time.

questions.text: What happens when the ball completely crosses the goal line between the posts and under the crossbar?
questions.explanation: A goal is scored when the whole of the ball passes over the goal line, between the goalposts, and under the crossbar.

questions.text: Which card does a referee show to send a player off the field?
questions.explanation: A red card results in the player being dismissed from the match. A yellow card is a caution; two yellows equal a red.

questions.text: What is awarded when an attacking player is fouled inside the opponent's penalty area?
questions.explanation: A penalty kick is awarded when a player commits a foul inside their own penalty area that would otherwise result in a direct free kick.

questions.text: From where is a penalty kick taken?
questions.explanation: Penalty kicks are taken from the penalty spot, which is 11 metres (12 yards) from the goal line and centred between the posts.

options.text: The penalty spot, 11 metres from goal
The edge of the penalty area

questions.text: When is a corner kick awarded?
questions.explanation: A corner kick is awarded when the ball wholly crosses the goal line (outside the goalposts) and was last touched by a defending player.

options.text: When the ball goes out over the goal line last touched by a defender
When the ball goes out over the touchline
When a goalkeeper saves the ball

questions.text: What is the maximum number of substitutions allowed per team in a standard competitive match?
questions.explanation: IFAB rules allow up to 5 substitutions per team in competitive matches (introduced in 2020). Individual competitions may set their own limits.

questions.text: What is a throw-in used to restart play?
questions.explanation: A throw-in is awarded to the opponents of the player who last touched the ball when the whole ball crosses the touchline.

options.text: When the ball crosses the touchline
When the ball crosses the goal line

questions.text: Which official is primarily responsible for enforcing the laws of the game on the field?
questions.explanation: The referee is the primary match official on the field, responsible for enforcing the Laws of the Game and controlling the match.
